question:
Solve the following percentages problem. Show your reasoning step by step.

Bobby can deadlift 300 pounds at 13.  When he is 18 he can deadlift 100 pounds more than 250% of his previous deadlift.  How many pounds did he add per year?
response:
Okay, so Bobby could deadlift 300 pounds at 13. At 18, it says he can lift 100 pounds more than 250% of his previous deadlift. I think 250% of 300 is, umm... 300 × 2.5? That would be 750 pounds. Then adding 100 makes it 850 pounds at 18.

Now, to figure out how much he added per year, I think I divide the difference (850 - 300 = 550) by the number of years (18 - 13 = 5). So 550 ÷ 5 is... 110 pounds per year? I think that’s right, but I’m not totally sure if I did the percentage part right.
